en: Doesn't matter, just get a load of this, all right?
If you get stopped by a cop in Amsterdam,
it's illegal for them to search you.
I mean, that's a right that cops in Amsterdam don't have.
ko: 즉, 암스테르담의 경찰은 그런 권리가 없어.

en: Oh man, I'm going, that's all it is to it. I'm fucking going.
ko: 아, 난 가고 있어, 그게 전부야. 난 정말 가고 있어.

en: I know, baby, you dig it the most.
ko: 알아, 자기야, 너가 제일 좋아하는 거야.

en: But you know what the funniest thing about Europe is? What?
ko: 그런데 유럽에서 가장 웃긴 점이 뭔지 알아? 뭐?

en: It's the little differences. I mean, they got the same shit over there that they got here, but it's just, just there, it's a little different. Example.
ko: 작은 차이점들이야. 거기에도 여기와 같은 것들이 있지만, 그냥 조금 달라. 예를 들어.

en: All right, well, you can walk into a movie theater
in Amsterdam and buy a beer.
And I don't mean just like, you know, a paper cup.
I'm talking about a glass of beer.
And in Paris, you can buy a beer in McDonald's.
ko: 그리고 파리에서는 맥도날드에서 맥주를 살 수 있어.

en: And you know what they call a quarter pounder
ko: 그리고 파리에서 그들이 쿼터 파운더를 뭐라고 부르는지 알아?

en: with cheese in Paris?
ko: 치즈가 들어간 쿼터 파운더라고 부르지 않아? 아니, 그들은 미터법을 사용해.

en: They don't call it a quarter pounder with cheese? No, man, they got the metric system.
ko: 그들은 쿼터 파운더가 뭔지 모를 거야. 아니, 그들은 미터법을 사용해.

en: They wouldn't know what the fuck a quarter pounder is. Well, what do they call it?
They call it a Royale with cheese. Royale with cheese. That's strange.
What do they call a Big Mac?
Big Mac's a Big Mac, but they call it Le Big Mac.
ko: 빅맥은 빅맥이지만, 그들은 르 빅맥이라고 불러.

en: What do they call a Wobble?
